segments.text: What if your mom had someone to talk to every day?
Good morning, Maggie! I've been thinking about you. Did Emma ever get the hang of that bicycle?
She did! You should have seen her, Sophie. She rode all the way down the driveway, no training wheels!
Oh, that's wonderful! I bet grandma was cheering from the porch.
You bet I was! I made her my famous dumplings to celebrate. She ate four of them!
Four! That's a new record. Those dumplings really are legendary, Maggie.
So tell me, how's that mystery novel going? Last time you said the detective was completely lost.
Oh, I finished it! And I was right — the butler didn't do it. Forty years as a librarian, you learn to spot the clues.
I love that! Have you picked up a new one yet?
Not yet. I've been a little tired this week, to be honest.
I'm sorry to hear that. Have you been sleeping okay? Sometimes a good cup of Earl Grey and an early night works wonders.
Lonesome No More. Companionship for your loved ones. Peace of mind for you.
